lines: India versus Pakistan. Economy only. Who wins?
GDP is the biggest gap. India is near 3 point 9 trillion dollars. Pakistan is around 372 billion.
GDP per person also favors India. About 2.7 thousand dollars versus 1.5 thousand.
Foreign exchange reserves are another major difference. India has far deeper buffers.
Exports also lean heavily toward India thanks to scale in services, manufacturing, and refined goods.
Stock market depth is not even close. India has one of the largest equity markets in the world.
Pakistan still matters regionally, but by economic scale and resilience, India is on another level.
That means more fiscal room, more import capacity, and more long-term strategic flexibility.
So by the economic numbers, India wins this battle comfortably.

seo.title: India vs Pakistan — Economy Gap by the Numbers 💰
seo.description:
India vs Pakistan economy comparison: GDP, GDP per capita, reserves, exports, and market depth.
